channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump taps his attorney Alina Habba to serve as counselor to the president
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has announced that he is appointing one of his defense attorneys in the New York hush money case as counselor to the president. Alina Habba, 40, defended Trump earlier this year, also serving as his legal spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US believes journalist Austin Tice is alive after disappearing in Syria in 2012, Biden says
item.description: President Joe Biden says the U.S. government believes missing American journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared 12 years ago near the Syrian capital, is alive and Washington is committed to bringing him home after Bashar Assad’s ouster from power.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump focuses on tariffs, not retribution, in first network TV interview
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump lays out plan for his first 100 days during interview with NBC News' "Meet the Press.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Trump, Prince William sit down for meeting in Paris after Notre Dame ceremony
item.description: President-elect Trump and Prince William shook hands at the reopening of Notre Dame in Paris on Saturday and later held a sit-down meeting at the British Embassy ahead of his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump allies chafe at Senate GOP resistance to nominees
item.description: “What a disgrace. If you’re a GOP Senator who voted for Lloyd Austin, but criticize @PeteHegseth, then maybe you’re in the wrong political party!” Trump Jr. posted on X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: National nonprofit asks Gov. McMaster to 'intervene' in South Carolina
item.description: A controversial Texas-based nonprofit promising to set up citizen patrols in McColl is now asking for state help.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says US should stay out of fighting in Syria as opposition forces gain
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says the U.S. should avoid engaging militarily in Syria, where an opposition offensive is threatening the rule of the country's Russian- and Iran-allied leader.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Congressman Torres blasts crime in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The cold-blooded shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in the heart of Manhattan shocked New Yorkers, including Congressman Ritchie Torres, leaving the lawmaker to pose the question: “What is happening in New York City?” Torres joined PIX on Politics to discuss city crim
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Mother of Austin Tice, journalist missing in Syria, says new information proves her son is alive
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The mother of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for more than a decade, said Friday that she was confident her son was alive, citing information she said had come from a “significant source” that she did not identify but said had been vetted by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis Mayor Frey poised to block council again as his veto use continues to climb
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has vetoed 15 actions since he first took office in 2018. That’s more vetoes than the previous two mayors combined.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Hegseth and says he believes he can be confirmed
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on women in combat.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NYC's mayor warms to Trump and doesn't rule out becoming a Republican
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams appears open to switching parties to become a Republican, as he declined to rule out a future change in political allegiances during a pair of interviews Friday that came as he has increasing warmed to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance tours Helene damage in North Carolina
item.description: The hurricane struck in late September and caused at least $53 billion in damage in North Carolina, according to a state government estimate.
item.dc:creator: Keleigh Beeson, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Ocasio-Cortez announces bid for top Oversight job as Democrats push for generational change
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez announced a bid Friday to become the top Democrat on the House Oversight Committee, the latest example of generational shakeup that is beginning to ripple across the Democratic Party in the wake of their election losses this year.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez seeks to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) on Friday formally launched a bid to lead Democrats on the Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress — a powerful seat that will play a prominent role in the party's effort to push back against President Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk funded a super PAC comparing Trump’s position on abortion to Ruth Bader Ginsburg’s
item.description: Elon Musk was the sole funder of a super PAC that focused on advertising likening Donald Trump’s stance on abortion to that of late Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump nominates Rodney Scott to lead Customs and Border Protection
item.description: Rodney Scott at CBP and Caleb Vitello at ICE would work alongside Stephen Miller, who was named deputy chief of staff for policy and Tom Homan, also tapped to be a "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota U.S. Attorney Andy Luger to step down ahead of Trump transition
item.description: U.S. Attorney Andy Luger is stepping down soon as the top federal law enforcement official in Minnesota. As is custom for political appointees, he’s resigning to make way for a yet-to-be-announced successor chosen by incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Trump names David Sacks as AI, crypto czar
item.description: President-elect Trump said Thursday that venture capitalist David Sacks will serve as the White House artificial intelligence (AI) and cryptocurrency czar.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Trump nominates former border chief Rodney Scott as commissioner of CBP
item.description: President-elect Trump nominated former border chief Rodney Scott as the commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Shouting match erupts over 9/11 during Secret Service hearing
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe and Rep. Pat Fallon (R-Texas) engaged in a shouting match that persisted over gavel banging, as Rowe accused the lawmaker of politicizing the Sept. 11 attack.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic rep pushes Biden to issue blanket pardon to those Patel could target
item.description: He noted that law enforcement officers, military personnel and others are at risk of extreme legal battles.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth slams 'manufactured media takedown' of him in WSJ op-ed
item.description: Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump's pick to lead the Defense Department, condemned the press for what he described as a "manufactured media takedown" of him. "The press is peddling anonymous story after anonymous story, all meant to smear me and tear me down.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: International students confused, anxious about their futures under second Trump term
item.description: International students are holding their breath as President-elect Trump gears up to return to the White House. Multiple schools have put out guidance on when foreign-born students should and shouldn't go abroad to ensure they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s mother says The New York Times made ‘threats’ by asking her to comment on a story
item.description: A basic tenet of journalism — calling someone for comment on a story — was seen as a threat by defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth’s mother.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump reportedly considering DeSantis as replacement nominee for defense secretary
item.description: Several reports, including from the Wall Street Journal, have said Governor Ron DeSantis could become President-elect Trump's new defense secretary nominee. The news comes amid a growing number of allegations against Trump's original nominee Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Inside RFK Jr.'s nonprofit's legal battles over vaccines and public health
item.description: The nonprofit Children's Health Defense that Kennedy led has filed nearly 30 federal and state lawsuits since 2020, many challenging vaccines and public health mandates.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk's more than $50 billion pay deal at Tesla was rejected again. Here is why
item.description: A judge in Delaware has for the second time struck down a compensation package for Elon Musk after a Tesla shareholder filed suit.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Unions score a major win in Wisconsin with a court ruling restoring collective bargaining rights
item.description: Wisconsin public worker and teachers unions have scored a major legal victory with a ruling that restores collective bargaining rights they lost under a 2011 state law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Wave of Democrats slam Biden's pardon of his son Hunter
item.description: While some Democrats extended a measure of sympathy, President Biden has taken heat on several fronts.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC
